title: secale cereale subsp. afghanicum
intro: Secale cereale subsp. afghanicum, also known as Afghan rye, is a subspecies of the rye plant that is native to Afghanistan. It is a perennial grass that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Afghan rye include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for various health conditions. Afghan rye can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
